name: Through the Horseshoes of the Urbasa and Andía Natural Park - circular from Maeztu
name_original: Por las Herraduras del Parque Natural de Urbasa y Andía — circular desde Maeztu
region_intro: Road cycling around Arraia-Maeztu features diverse terrain, from the gentle gradients of the Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro to more challenging mountain passes. The region is characterized by river valleys, such as the Berrón, and the surrounding Montaña Alavesa, offering varied landscapes for cyclists. Former railway lines provide smooth, well-maintained paths, while other routes explore the natural features of the Álava province. Best road cycling routes around Arraia-Maeztu The most popular road cycling route is Through the Horseshoes of the Urbasa and Andía Natural Park - circular from Maeztu, a 78.1 miles (125.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Opakua Pass – Alto de Iturrieta (990 m) loop from Arabako Mendialdea, a moderate 45.0 miles (72.4 km) path. This loop includes climbs over the Opakua Pass, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Azáceta – Bernedo Village loop from Antoñana, a 55.2 miles (88.9 km) trail leading through mountain passes and villages, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Arraia-Maeztu is defined by river valleys, former railway lines, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier rides along the Vía Verde to more challenging climbs. The routes in Arraia-Maeztu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arraia-Maeztu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: There are over 75 road cycling routes around Arraia-Maeztu, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: Road cycling in Arraia-Maeztu features diverse terrain, from the gentle gradients of the Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro to more challenging mountain passes. You'll encounter picturesque river valleys, such as the Berrón, and the scenic Montaña Alavesa, providing varied landscapes for cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: Yes, Arraia-Maeztu offers 9 easy road cycling routes. The Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro is particularly suitable for all users, providing a smooth and easily rideable surface, often following the Berrón river through tranquil valleys and even short, well-maintained tunnels.

Q: What about more challenging road cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 32 difficult road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035138851/through-the-horseshoes-of-the-urbasa-and-andia-natural-park-circular-from-maeztu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the Horseshoes of the Urbasa and Andía Natural Park - circular from Maeztu&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 125.7 km with significant elevation gain through the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5349873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Opakua Pass – Alto de Iturrieta (990 m) loop from Arabako Mendialdea&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 72.4 km path that includes climbs over the Opakua Pass, offering scenic views.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while road cycling in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: Along the routes, especially the Vía Verde, you can discover historical and natural attractions. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igoroin Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4408039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murallas de Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Joar Summit (1,414 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The Vía Verde Interpretation Center in Antoñana also offers insights into the old railway line's history.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the smooth Vía Verde to challenging mountain passes, and the beautiful, rich landscapes of the Álava province.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through villages or offer opportunities for stops?
A: Many routes connect villages, providing opportunities for breaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31504980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Azáceta – Bernedo Village loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt; is an 88.9 km trail that leads through mountain passes and villages, perfect for exploring local amenities.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of natural parks?
A: Absolutely. The region is close to natural parks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035138851/through-the-horseshoes-of-the-urbasa-and-andia-natural-park-circular-from-maeztu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Through the Horseshoes of the Urbasa and Andía Natural Park - circular from Maeztu&lt;/a&gt; route takes you directly through the Urbasa and Andía Natural Park, showcasing its unique landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: Arraia-Maeztu offers beautiful terrain for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is also popular, though some routes might be more exposed to the sun. Winter cycling is possible, but conditions can vary, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that feature historical railway infrastructure?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Ferrocarril Vasco-Navarro is built on a former railway line and features impressive engineering from its past, including wooden bridges crossing roads and a series of bridges over the Berrón river. This route offers a unique blend of cycling and historical exploration.

Q: Where can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Arraia-Maeztu?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20162887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Martín – Aterpe Kampezu Hostel loop from Antoñana&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 92 km. The Vía Verde itself is an extensive route, and segments can be combined for longer distances, connecting Vitoria-Gasteiz with Estella.
tips: From Salvatierra, a small road leads to the port of Opakua, with spectacular views over the Urbasa mountain range, in the nearby lands of Navarra. The views are awesome!
This section of road with 5 horseshoes and amazing panoramic views is a true delight for the cyclist palate. You will have magnificent views as you go up towards the entire Urbasa-Andia Natural Park.
